degraded.detail: classify_complexity flagged touches_migrations from message prose only ('database'/'migrations' narrative); overridden -- the diff has zero SQL / supabase/migrations paths. F11 recomputes risk flags from the diff.

shared/tests/test_campaign_status_backfill.py::test_existing_campaign_regenerates_without_downgrade[2026-07-15-test-traceability-layers] fails identically on clean origin/main (confirmed via stash). Root cause is the campaign-status skeleton parser mis-reading the LOCAL, gitignored campaign.md (0 tracked files) -- CI never sees this campaign. Not a TT1 regression; out of TT1 scope (collector + templates).

surface_verification.justification: TT1 ships a compliance data collector (test_links) that emits a generated JSON artifact, the @FR tag convention in two rule templates, and their tests. There is no startable, user-experienceable surface (no web/API/CLI app affordance) and no backend-affects-frontend change (detect_frontend_changes: has_frontend_changes=false). Fully exercised by the compliance + shared pytest suites at F0 (compliance 1009 GREEN, shared 4039 GREEN) plus empirical boundary/round-trip probes recorded in the iterate ADR.

behaviors.behavior: AC1 manifest maps FRs -> {unit,integration,e2e} test lists from @FR tags across pytest+Playwright+Vitest and equals the frozen golden answer key (bindings, tag_source, orphans, invalid, untagged)
AC1/AC4 the emitted manifest validates against the v2 JSON Schema and schema_version==2, collector_version + spec_hash present
AC1 spec_path is project-root-relative POSIX (rooting pinned directly, not normalized away)
AC1/R1 skipped != covered: an FR whose only e2e test is skipped has coverage.e2e=MISSING; ok requires enabled+pass
AC2 a describe/suite-level tag applies to every inner test (same-line, multiline callback brace, and nested describes)
AC2 multiple tags per test are supported (suite tag + own title-suffix tag both bind)
AC2 a malformed tag is recorded in invalid_tags, never crashes
AC3 an untagged test lands in untagged_tests; a malformed-tagged test does NOT (it is a hygiene finding)
AC3 a test tagged to a removed FR is a confirmed_orphan (reason fr_removed); the removed FR keeps no live tests
AC4 the manifest regenerates through the real update_compliance CLI (--phase iterate emits test-traceability.json)
AC4 generate_file writes a schema-valid manifest over conventional roots; the bounded-scope boundary (a stray root-level test is not scanned) is pinned
Layer detection: integration-tests/ dir + tests/integration/ + .integration.test.* -> integration; e2e/ + .spec -> e2e; else unit (fixes the review's HIGH misclassification bug)
FR parser: 4-col traceability shape reads explicit Layers + provenance (explicit / inferred_legacy UI-flow->e2e / defaulted_legacy->unit)
FR parser: a ## Removed Requirements row parses with status=removed; active rows stay active
FR parser: the 5-col adopt shape (ADR-031) reads the Description as the title, NOT as Layers, and infers layers
FR parser: manifest key is namespace::FR-XX.YY and spec_path is carried (frozen requirement_model consumed, not forked)
AC5 the @FR tag convention is documented in the tests + integration-tests rule templates; all new source/test modules <=300 LOC; bloat baseline not ratcheted
behaviors.evidence: structural: git diff shows both rules/*.template carry the '@FR' convention section; wc -l = 211/153/160/84 (collectors) + 299/89 (tests), all <=300; shipwright_bloat_baseline.json unchanged in the diff
